Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The user wants me to generate a response based on the provided scenario I need to Identify anomalies in the temporal profile model from the provided data Generate hypotheses for why these anomalies exist Propose SQL verification queries

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Key anomalies from the provided model RP Very low STDEV 3600 seconds 1 hour compared to the average 90000 seconds about 25 hours This is suspiciously rigid PN Very high average 604800 seconds 7 days with high STDEV 172800 2 days Long delay with high variability AC Short time 7200 seconds 2 hours might indicate skipping steps EN Very short time 300 seconds 5 minutes might be automated or skipped steps RE Average 1 day 86400 with STDEV 8 hours 28800 seems reasonable but contextually might need checking against RP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Important SQL considerations Need to calculate time differences between activities for the same claim_id Need to join with claims table to get claim_type etc Need to join with adjusters if looking at adjuster patterns though adjuster_id is not in claim_events it is in the resource column or need to infer from assignment Actually looking at the schema claim_events has a resource column which likely contains adjuster_id or name The adjuster assignment is activity A so the resource for A would be the adjuster

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Anomalies RP Unusually tight standard deviation 1 hour relative to mean about 25 hours suggesting artificially rigid scheduling PN Extreme latency 7 days average with high variance 2 days indicating inconsistent notification workflows AC Rapid closure 2 hours potentially bypassing evaluation approval stages EN Suspiciously rapid transition 5 minutes suggesting automation or missing intermediate verification steps EC High variance 50 minutes STDEV vs 60 min mean relative to mean indicating inconsistent closure practices post evaluation

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Hypotheses RP rigidity Automated approval systems with fixed scheduling or batch processing at specific intervals PN delay Manual notification processes prone to backlogs or external communication delays mail vs email or resource constraints in customer service teams AC speed Straight through processing for low value claims or erroneous closures or fraud quick closure to avoid scrutiny EN speed Automated system notifications without human review or data quality issues timestamps recorded incorrectly

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Query 1 Find claims with RP deviation rigid timing Calculate Z score or just find those outside reasonable bounds Query 2 Find claims with extreme PN delays Look for more than 7 days plus 2 STDEV or similar Query 3 Find AC without intermediate steps Check if claims closed quickly after assignment missing E or P Query 4 Correlate with claim types adjusters Join with claims table and group by claim_type or adjuster Query 5 EN rapid transitions Find those under 1 minute or similar

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For time differences I need self joins or window functions LAG LEAD functions would be efficient

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Or better pivot the activities This pivot approach is cleaner for specific pair analysis

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now for correlation with adjusters The adjuster is assigned during activity A so the resource column for activity A contains the adjuster_id or name

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Double check the anomaly list RP Low STDEV 3600 vs Mean 90000 yes suspiciously rigid PN High mean 604800 and high STDEV 172800 yes 7 days avg 2 days stddev AC Short time 7200 yes 2 hours EN Short time 300 yes 5 minutes EC High STDEV 3000 vs Mean 3600 yes coefficient of variation is high almost 1
